prompt: Create a dreamy vertical anime-style illustration of a lavender-themed dessert café scene. A young woman with short wavy violet-gray bob hair and a small side braid sits at a sunlit table in front of tall windows, wearing a delicate lavender camisole dress with lace trim and two ribbon bow straps. Her face is intentionally hidden by a centered opaque square censor block in a muted mauve-to-pale-pink gradient, covering the entire face area while leaving hair, neck, shoulders, and outfit visible. She holds one hand near her cheek and the other rests near a giant bowl of shaved ice. The dessert is the focal point: an oversized pastel pink and purple kakigori mound in a clear cut-glass bowl on a saucer, topped with one lavender ice cream scoop, crystalline sugar shards, edible glitter, small star decorations, a large white snowflake decoration on the front, and blue-purple jelly or crystal pieces around the base; place a small gold spoon on the saucer. On the left side of the table, include a printed café menu card in Japanese with the brand name “SETSUGETSU HYOKA” and the main item “月光のラベンダー氷 ～星屑のきらめき～”, followed by exactly 6 bullet items: “ラベンダーシロップ”, “バタフライピージュレ”, “ミルクエスプーマ”, “ホワイトチョコスノー”, “パールクラッシュ”, “星型の琥珀糖”; show the price “¥2,200（税込）” and a short Japanese description below. In the softly blurred background, include a wall sign with the café name 雪月氷菓, lavender and hydrangea flower arrangements, sheer curtains, warm afternoon sunlight, sparkling dust motes, and a pastel purple-pink color palette. Add table details: small glass dishes with sugar crystals, scattered lavender and blue gems, a glowing round glass lamp on the right, and tiny flowers in vases. Use high-detail polished anime rendering, luminous backlighting, soft bloom, transparent glass reflections, delicate linework, ethereal summer atmosphere, no extra characters, no watermark. Customizable elements: dessert name 月光のラベンダー氷 ～星屑のきらめき～, price ¥2,200（税込）, café name 雪月氷菓, dress color lavender, censor block color muted mauve to pale pink gradient.
